mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates strong consumer spending, directly boosting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as the demand channel reinforces economic expansion.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return after a monthly RSAFS print above its 12-month moving average is not statistically positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) over any rolling 5-year window; or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals impending labor market weakness that reduces aggregate demand and depresses corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: If UNRATE crosses above 5.5%, the average 60-day forward return is not negative, or the proportion of negative returns is below 55% over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous tightening of monetary policy (rising Fed funds rate) and weakening housing demand (declining housing starts) jointly signal a contraction in interest‑sensitive sectors, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by at least 25bp over 3 months while HOUST decreases by at least 5% over the same period
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with housing weakness
failure_condition: In episodes where the trigger condition holds, the median 20‑day forward return is not negative, or the hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth provides a positive signal for S&P 500 returns during expansionary regimes (real GDP growth above 2%) as it reflects healthy supply capacity and margins; its effect turns negative in recessions when excess supply depresses margins. This regime‑conditional mechanism is currently positive in the mid‑cycle expansion.
failure_condition: In periods where real GDP growth exceeds 2%, the rank IC of INDPRO changes predicting 60‑day forward returns is below 0.03, or in recession periods the sign does not flip to negative (i.e., IC does not become negative with p < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When year‑over‑year CPI inflation rises above 2.5%, it triggers expectations of Federal Reserve tightening, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 2.5%, the 20‑day forward returns are not significantly negative (one‑sided t‑test p > 0.05) compared to all other periods, or the median return is not lower than the unconditional median.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below 1.5 million annualized units signals a material slowdown in residential investment, a key leading indicator for economic contractions, which depresses corporate earnings and stock prices over the following quarter.
failure_condition: After a monthly HOUST print below 1500, the subsequent 60‑day forward return is positive in more than 40% of cases, or the average return is not negative (one‑sided p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production posts strong monthly gains but payroll employment growth remains very weak, the divergence signals productivity gains not translating into hiring, indicating an unsustainable recovery that leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.5% and PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 0.1% over a 3-month rolling window
failure_condition: If the joint condition holds, the average 20‑day forward return is not negative, or the proportion of negative returns is below 55% over any 3‑year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll employment gains above 200,000 signal robust labor demand and rising household income, which immediately boosts consumption expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following week as markets respond to improving macro fundamentals.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS monthly change is above 200,000, the average 5‑day forward return is not positive, or the win rate (positive days) is below 55% across all occurrences.
